rulings.Case Title: JOHN H. CARDOZA et al VS. 3M COMPANY, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS PARENT, ALTER EGO, et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, December 8, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 7. Defendant Union Carbide Corporation¿s motion for summary judgment: hearing required. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: LYNN PATRICK SCHMIDT VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, December 8, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Metalclad Insulation Corporation's motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1 (general negligence excluding negligent failure to warn) and 3 (strict liability design and manufacturing defect). Assuming without deciding that defendant satisfied its burden of production by presenting admissible evidence sufficient to establish the first two elements of Boyle and plaintiff did not present any evidence creating a triable issue on either of those elements, defendant did not present admissible evidence sufficient to establish the third element of Boyle. Plaintiffs' evidentiary objections 22-37 are sustained. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: LYNN PATRICK SCHMIDT VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, December 8, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Metalclad Insulation Corporation's motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1 (general negligence excluding negligent failure to warn) and 3 (strict liability design and manufacturing defect). Assuming without deciding that defendant satisfied its burden of production by presenting admissible evidence sufficient to establish the first two elements of Boyle and plaintiff did not present any evidence creating a triable issue on either of those elements, defendant did not present admissible evidence sufficient to establish the third element of Boyle. Plaintiffs' evidentiary objections 22-37 are sustained. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: GILBERT FORBES VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, December 8, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 8. Defendant Union Carbide Corp.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its burden of production and plaintiff failed to submit admissible evidence creating a triable issue whether he was exposed to raw asbestos supplied by defendant. Defendant¿s objections to the sealed documents on grounds of lack of authentication are sustained. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: BRIAN ROBERTS et al VS. ALFA LAVAL INC SUED INDIVIDUALLLY AND AS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, December 8, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Welco Manufacturing Company¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant did not present sufficient admissible evidence to satisfy its burden of production that it did not manufacture, sell, supply or distribute the Welco joint compound that Mr. Roberts was exposed to. Plaintiffs¿ objections to paragraphs 7 and 12 of the declaration of Mr. Strader are sustained. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: GERRY LEE STONE VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, December 8, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Metalclad Insulation Corporation¿s motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1 (general negligence excluding negligent failure to warn) and 3 (strict liability design and manufacturing defect), granted as to issue 6 (premises owner/contractor liability) and taken off calendar as to issues 5 and 7 (false representation and punitive damages). Assuming without deciding that defendant satisfied its burden of production by presenting admissible evidence sufficient to establish the first two elements of Boyle and plaintiff did not present any evidence creating a triable issue on either of those elements, defendant did not present admissible evidence sufficient to establish the third element of Boyle. Plaintiffs¿ evidentiary objections 22-37 are sustained. Plaintiff states that he dismissed his claim for false representation and request for punitive damages and appears to concede the lack of merit of his claim for premises owner/contractor liability. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DONALD GAUGER et al VS. ALCATEL LUCENT et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, December 8, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant Rockwell Automation, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is granted and plaintiffs' request for a continuance is denied. Defendant satisfied its burden of production and plaintiffs did not present any evidence creating a triable issue that Mr. Gauger was exposed to any asbestos-containing product attributable to defendant. Plaintiffs' supplemental interrogatory responses which were served after the date the motion was filed are not considered in determining whether defendant met its burden of production. Plaintiffs have failed to show that either the continued deposition of Mr. Ho or the deposition of defendant's custodian of records might provide facts to oppose this motion. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: GLORIA REA et al VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, December 8, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 5. Defendant Power Industries, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its burden of production and plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether Mr. Rea ever worked with or around gaskets or gasket material supplied by defendant. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: KENNETH MCGREW VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, December 8, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 6. Defendant Campbell Soup Company¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issue 1 (duty) and granted as to issue 2 (punitive damages). The deposition and declaration of Mr. Cantley creates a triable fact whether Mr. McGrew was exposed to asbestos-containing debris disturbed by defendant. Defendant did not satisfy its burden of production on the issue of its actual or constructive knowledge of asbestos hazards due to its failure to propound contention discovery regarding its constructive knowledge. Every party who disturbs asbestos-containing debris in the vicinity of an individual has a duty to use reasonable care not to cause or contribute to an asbestos-related illness of the individual as a result of the party¿s disturbing the asbestos-containing debris. Plaintiffs appear to concede the lack of merit of their request for punitive damages. = (220/HEK)
